{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Gst-nvstreamdemux Pipeline Example (x86)\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/text/DS_plugin_gst-nvstreamdemux\\n\\nThis GStreamer pipeline demonstrates the use of nvstreammux, nvinfer, nvstreamdemux, and nvdsosd on an x86 system. It configures two input streams, batches them, performs inference, then demuxes the results for display on separate windows using nvdsosd and nveglglessink.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\ngst-launch-1.0 -e nvstreammux name=mux batch-size=2 width=1920 height=1080 ! nvinfer config-file-path=/opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/configs/deepstream-app/config_infer_primary.txt batch-size=2\\n! nvstreamdemux name=demux filesrc location=/opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4 !qtdemux ! h264parse ! nvv4l2decoder ! queue ! \\nmux.sink_0 filesrc location=/opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_720p.h264 ! h264parse ! nvv4l2decoder !\\nqueue ! mux.sink_1 demux.src_0 ! \\\"video/x-raw(memory:NVMM), format=NV12\\\" ! queue ! nvvideoconvert ! \\\"video/x-raw(memory:NVMM), format=RGBA\\\" ! nvdsosd !\\nnvvideoconvert ! nveglglessink demux.src_1 ! queue ! \\\"video/x-raw(memory:NVMM), format=NV12\\\" ! queue ! nvvideoconvert !\\n\\\"video/x-raw(memory:NVMM), format=RGBA\\\" ! nvdsosd ! nveglglessink\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### GStreamer Plugins for Inference\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/sdk-api/classdeepstream_1_1NvDsTilerEventHandler\\n\\nDocumentation for DeepStream's GStreamer plugins used for performing inference, including &#96;gstnvinfer&#96; and &#96;nvinfer1&#96;.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;APIDOC\\n## GStreamer Plugins for Inference\\n\\n### Description\\nThis section details the GStreamer plugins provided by DeepStream for performing AI inference on video streams.\\n\\n### gstnvinfer Plugin\\n\\n#### Title\\ngstnvinfer\\n\\n#### Description\\nA GStreamer plugin that performs inference using NVIDIA's TensorRT. It supports various models and configurations for object detection, classification, segmentation, etc.\\n\\n#### Method\\nElement\\n\\n#### Endpoint\\nN/A (GStreamer Plugin)\\n\\n#### Parameters\\n\\n##### Properties\\n- **config-file-path** (string) - Required - Path to the configuration file for the inference engine.\\n- **model-engine-file** (string) - Optional - Path to the pre-built TensorRT engine file. If not provided, the engine will be built from the model files specified in the config file.\\n- **batch-size** (integer) - Optional - The batch size for inference. Defaults to the model's maximum batch size.\\n- **process-mode** (integer) - Optional - The mode of processing (e.g., &#96;1&#96; for full inference, &#96;2&#96; for detection only). Defaults to &#96;1&#96;.\\n- **unique-id** (integer) - Required - A unique identifier for this inference element in the pipeline.\\n- **operate-on-gie-id** (integer) - Optional - GIE ID to operate on. Useful when multiple GIEs are present.\\n\\n### nvinfer1 API\\n\\n#### Title\\nnvinfer1\\n\\n#### Description\\nLow-level C API for performing inference, often used internally by &#96;gstnvinfer&#96; or for custom applications.\\n\\n#### Method\\nAPI\\n\\n#### Endpoint\\nN/A (C API)\\n\\n#### Functions\\n\\n- **NvInferContextInitParams** (struct) - Structure for initializing the inference context.\\n- **_GstNvInferClass** (struct) - Class structure for the &#96;gstnvinfer&#96; element.\\n\\n### Request Example (gstnvinfer)\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\ngst-launch-1.0 filesrc location=video.mp4 ! qtdemux ! h264parse ! nvv4l2decoder ! m.sink_0 nvstreammux name=m batch-size=4 width=1280 height=720 ! \\n    nvinfer config-file-path=/path/to/config_infer.txt batch-size=4 unique-id=1 ! \\n    nvdsosd ! nvv4l2h264enc ! filesink location=output.h264\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### Response Example (gstnvinfer)\\nN/A (GStreamer plugin outputs processed frames or metadata downstream).\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### DeepStream NvInfer and NvTracker Components\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/sdk-api/classnvdsinfer_1_1FullDimTrtBackendContext\\n\\nDetails on NvInfer related structures for inference parameters and outputs, and NvTracker components for object tracking, including GstNvInferFrame, GstNvInferObjectInfo, and GstNvTrackerMiscDataObject.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;APIDOC\\n## DeepStream NvInfer and NvTracker Components\\n\\n### Description\\n\\nStructures and classes related to NVIDIA's inference (NvInfer) and tracking (NvTracker) components within DeepStream, used for processing model outputs and tracking objects across frames.\\n\\n### Classes/Structs\\n\\n- **GstNvInferFrame**: Represents a frame processed by NvInfer.\\n- **GstNvInferObjectInfo**: Information about an object detected by NvInfer.\\n- **GstNvStreamMuxCtx**: Context for the NvStreamMux element.\\n- **GstNvTrackerMiscDataObject**: Miscellaneous data object for NvTracker.\\n- **INvTrackerProc**: Interface for tracker processing.\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### NvDsMeta API\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/python-api/PYTHON_API/NvDsTrackerMeta/NvDsObjReid\\n\\nThis section covers the NvDsMeta (DeepStream Metadata) classes used for managing metadata associated with frames, objects, and other elements in the DeepStream pipeline. Includes base metadata, batch metadata, frame metadata, object metadata, and more.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;APIDOC\\n## NvDsMeta Classes\\n\\n### Description\\nClasses for handling and managing DeepStream metadata, including information about frames, objects, classifications, and user-defined data.\\n\\n### Classes\\n- **NvDsMetaType**\\n- **NvDsComp_BboxInfo**\\n  - &#96;NvDsComp_BboxInfo.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsMetaPool**\\n  - &#96;NvDsMetaPool.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsBaseMeta**\\n  - &#96;NvDsBaseMeta.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsBatchMeta**\\n  - &#96;NvDsBatchMeta.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsFrameMeta**\\n  - &#96;NvDsFrameMeta.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsObjectMeta**\\n  - &#96;NvDsObjectMeta.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsClassifierMeta**\\n  - &#96;NvDsClassifierMeta.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsLabelInfo**\\n  - &#96;NvDsLabelInfo.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsDisplayMeta**\\n  - &#96;NvDsDisplayMeta.cast()&#96;\\n- **NvDsUserMeta**\\n  - &#96;NvDsUserMeta.cast()&#96;\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Input Stream Handling with Decoding and Transfer\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/text/DS_plugin_gst-nvdsxfer\\n\\nThis section of the pipeline configures the input streams, including reading H.265 files, parsing them, decoding using &#96;nvv4l2decoder&#96; on specified GPUs, and potentially transferring data between GPUs using &#96;nvdsxfer&#96;. This is repeated for multiple sink pads (&#96;m.sink_0&#96; through &#96;m.sink_7&#96;) connected to a &#96;nvstreammux&#96;.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=0 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! m.sink_0\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=0 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! m.sink_1\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=0 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! m.sink_2\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=0 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! m.sink_3\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=1 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! nvdsxfer gpu-id=0 p2p_gpu_id=1 ! queue ! m.sink_4\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=1 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! nvdsxfer gpu-id=0 p2p_gpu_id=1 ! queue ! m.sink_5\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=1 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! nvdsxfer gpu-id=0 p2p_gpu_id=1 ! queue ! m.sink_6\\nmultifilesrc location=samples/streams/sample_1080p.h265 loop=true ! h265parse ! queue ! nvv4l2decoder gpu-id=1 cudadec-memtype=0 ! queue ! nvdsxfer gpu-id=0 p2p_gpu_id=1 ! queue ! m.sink_7 -e -v\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\"}]}"
}

{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Build and Run Deepstream GStreamer Pipeline\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/notebooks/deepstream_launchpad.ipynb\\n\\nThis function initializes GStreamer, creates and configures essential pipeline elements (nvstreammux, nvinfer, nvtracker), adds them to the pipeline, and links them appropriately. It handles input source creation, inference engine configuration, and optional tracker setup. Dependencies include GStreamer libraries, sys, and configparser.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\ndef build_and_run_pipeline():\\n    # Standard initialization procedure\\n    Gst.init(None)\\n\\n    # Create gstreamer elements\\n    # Create Pipeline element that will form a connection of other elements\\n    print(\\\"Creating Pipeline \\\\n \\\")\\n    pipeline = Gst.Pipeline()\\n\\n    if not pipeline:\\n        sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create Pipeline \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    number_sources = len(input_file_list) # Declare number of input sources\\n    \\n    print(\\\"Creating streammux \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n    # Create nvstreammux instance to form batches from one or more sources.\\n    streammux = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvstreammux\\\", \\\"Stream-muxer\\\")\\n    if not streammux:\\n        sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create NvStreamMux \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    # Set streammux properties\\n    streammux.set_property('width', 1920)\\n    streammux.set_property('height', 1080)\\n    streammux.set_property('batch-size', number_sources)\\n    streammux.set_property('batched-push-timeout', 4000000)\\n\\n    pipeline.add(streammux) # Add streammux to the pipeline\\n    \\n    # Iterate through each input source\\n    for i in range(number_sources):\\n        print(\\\"Creating source_bin \\\",i,\\\" \\\\n \\\")\\n        uri_name=input_file_list[i] # Retrieve input file URI\\n        if uri_name.find(\\\"rtsp://\\\") == 0 :\\n            is_live = True\\n        source_bin=create_source_bin(i, uri_name) # Call helper to create source bin\\n        if not source_bin:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\"Unable to create source bin \\\\n\\\")\\n        pipeline.add(source_bin) # Add source bin to pipeline\\n        padname=\\\"sink_%u\\\" %i\\n        sinkpad= streammux.request_pad_simple(padname) # Retrieve a sink pad from the streammux element\\n        if not sinkpad:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\"Unable to create sink pad bin \\\\n\\\")\\n        srcpad=source_bin.get_static_pad(\\\"src\\\") # Retrieve the source pad of the source bin\\n        if not srcpad:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\"Unable to create src pad bin \\\\n\\\")\\n        srcpad.link(sinkpad) # Link the source pad of the source bin to the sink pad of the streammux\\n        \\n        print(\\\"Creating nvinfer (PGIE) \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n    # Create nvinfer element\\n    pgie = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvinfer\\\", \\\"primary-inference\\\")\\n    if not pgie:\\n        sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create pgie \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    # Set nvinfer properties\\n    pgie.set_property('config-file-path', pgie_config_file)\\n    pgie_batch_size=pgie.get_property(\\\"batch-size\\\")\\n\\n    if(pgie_batch_size != number_sources):\\n        print(\\\"WARNING: Overriding infer-config batch-size\\\",pgie_batch_size,\\\" with number of sources \\\", number_sources,\\\" \\\\n\\\")\\n        pgie.set_property(\\\"batch-size\\\",number_sources)\\n\\n\\n    # Add pgie to pipeline\\n    pipeline.add(pgie)\\n    \\n    if enable_tracker:\\n        print(\\\"Creating nvtracker\\\\n \\\")\\n\\n        # Create nvtracker element\\n        tracker = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvtracker\\\", \\\"tracker\\\")\\n        if not tracker:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create tracker \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n        # Parse tracker config file and set properties\\n        config = configparser.ConfigParser()\\n        config.read(tracker_config_file)\\n        config.sections()\\n\\n        for key in config['tracker']:\\n            if key == 'tracker-width' :\\n                tracker_width = config.getint('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('tracker-width', tracker_width)\\n            if key == 'tracker-height' :\\n                tracker_height = config.getint('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('tracker-height', tracker_height)\\n            if key == 'gpu-id' :\\n                tracker_gpu_id = config.getint('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('gpu_id', tracker_gpu_id)\\n            if key == 'll-lib-file' :\\n                tracker_ll_lib_file = config.get('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('ll-lib-file', tracker_ll_lib_file)\\n            if key == 'll-config-file' :\\n                tracker_ll_config_file = config.get('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('ll-config-file', tracker_ll_config_file)\\n\\n        # Add tracker to pipeline\\n        pipeline.add(tracker)\\n        \\n        print(\\\"Creating nvinfer (SGIE1) \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n    \\n        # Create nvinfer element\\n        sgie1 = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvinfer\\\", \\\"secondary1-nvinference-engine\\\")\\n        if not sgie1:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to make sgie1 \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n        print(\\\"Creating nvinfer (SGIE2) \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n        # Create nvinfer element\\n        sgie2 = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvinfer\\\", \\\"secondary2-nvinference-engine\\\")\\n        if not sgie2:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to make sgie2 \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n        # Set config file for each SGIE\\n        sgie1.set_property('config-file-path', sgie1_config_file)\\n        sgie2.set_property('config-file-path', sgie2_config_file)\\n\\n        # Add SGIEs to pipeline\\n        pipeline.add(sgie1)\\n        pipeline.add(sgie2)\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Python Probe Function for DeepStream Metadata Processing\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/notebooks/deepstream_launchpad.ipynb\\n\\nThis Python function, designed for DeepStream's tiler sink pad, processes buffer metadata to extract and visualize object detection results. It iterates through frames and objects, customizing bounding box colors and adding text overlays with frame and object counts. Dependencies include the &#96;pyds&#96; module for DeepStream metadata access.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\ndef tiler_sink_pad_buffer_probe(pad,info,u_data):\\n    frame_number=0\\n    #Intiallizing object counter with 0.\\n    obj_counter = {\\n        PGIE_CLASS_ID_VEHICLE:0,\\n        PGIE_CLASS_ID_PERSON:0,\\n        PGIE_CLASS_ID_BICYCLE:0,\\n        PGIE_CLASS_ID_ROADSIGN:0\\n    }\\n    num_rects=0\\n    gst_buffer = info.get_buffer()\\n    if not gst_buffer:\\n        print(\\\"Unable to get GstBuffer \\\")\\n        return\\n\\n    # Retrieve batch metadata from the gst_buffer\\n    # Note that pyds.gst_buffer_get_nvds_batch_meta() expects the\\n    # C address of gst_buffer as input, which is obtained with hash(gst_buffer)\\n    batch_meta = pyds.gst_buffer_get_nvds_batch_meta(hash(gst_buffer))\\n    l_frame = batch_meta.frame_meta_list\\n    while l_frame is not None:\\n        try:\\n            # Note that l_frame.data needs a cast to pyds.NvDsFrameMeta\\n            # The casting is done by pyds.NvDsFrameMeta.cast()\\n            # The casting also keeps ownership of the underlying memory\\n            # in the C code, so the Python garbage collector will leave\\n            # it alone.\\n            frame_meta = pyds.NvDsFrameMeta.cast(l_frame.data)\\n        except StopIteration:\\n            break\\n\\n        frame_number=frame_meta.frame_num\\n        num_rects = frame_meta.num_obj_meta\\n        l_obj=frame_meta.obj_meta_list\\n        while l_obj is not None:\\n            try:\\n                # Casting l_obj.data to pyds.NvDsObjectMeta\\n                obj_meta=pyds.NvDsObjectMeta.cast(l_obj.data)\\n            except StopIteration:\\n                break\\n            obj_counter[obj_meta.class_id] += 1 # Increment object counter\\n            rectparams = obj_meta.rect_params # Retrieve bounding box parameters\\n            # Set color of bbox. (red, green, blue, alpha), 0.0 \u003c= RGBA \u003c= 1.0\\n            rectparams.border_color.set(bbox_border_color[\\\"R\\\"], bbox_border_color[\\\"G\\\"], bbox_border_color[\\\"B\\\"], bbox_border_color[\\\"A\\\"])\\n            # Set whether bbox has background color\\n            rectparams.has_bg_color = bbox_has_bg_color\\n            # If bbox has background color, set background color\\n            if bbox_has_bg_color:\\n                rectparams.bg_color.set(bbox_bg_color[\\\"R\\\"], bbox_bg_color[\\\"G\\\"], bbox_bg_color[\\\"B\\\"], bbox_bg_color[\\\"A\\\"])\\n            try: \\n                l_obj=l_obj.next\\n            except StopIteration:\\n                break\\n\\n        # Acquiring a display meta object. The memory ownership remains in\\n        # the C code so downstream plugins can still access it. Otherwise\\n        # the garbage collector will claim it when this probe function exits.\\n        display_meta=pyds.nvds_acquire_display_meta_from_pool(batch_meta)\\n        display_meta.num_labels = 1\\n        py_nvosd_text_params = display_meta.text_params[0]\\n        # Setting display text to be shown on screen\\n        # Note that the pyds module allocates a buffer for the string, and the\\n        # memory will not be claimed by the garbage collector.\\n        # Reading the display_text field here will return the C address of the\\n        # allocated string. Use pyds.get_string() to get the string content.\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.display_text = \\\"Frame Number={}\\\" Number of Objects={} Vehicle_count={} Person_count={}\\\".format(frame_number, num_rects, obj_counter[PGIE_CLASS_ID_VEHICLE], obj_counter[PGIE_CLASS_ID_PERSON])\\n\\n        # Now set the offsets where the string should appear\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.x_offset = text_x_offset\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.y_offset = text_y_offset\\n\\n        # Font, font-color and font-size\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.font_params.font_name = text_font_name\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.font_params.font_size = text_font_size\\n        # set(red, green, blue, alpha); set to White\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.font_params.font_color.set(text_font_color[\\\"R\\\"], text_font_color[\\\"G\\\"], text_font_color[\\\"B\\\"], text_font_color[\\\"A\\\"])\\n\\n        # Text background color\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.set_bg_clr = text_set_bg_color\\n        # set(red, green, blue, alpha); set to Black\\n        py_nvosd_text_params.text_bg_clr.set(text_bg_color[\\\"R\\\"], text_bg_color[\\\"G\\\"], text_bg_color[\\\"B\\\"], text_bg_color[\\\"A\\\"])\\n        # Using pyds.get_string() to get display_text as string\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Create and Configure nvinfer (PGIE) Element for Inference\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/notebooks/deepstream_launchpad.ipynb\\n\\nThis code sets up the Gst-nvinfer plugin, acting as the Primary General Inference Engine (PGIE). It is configured via a file path to perform object detection and classification. The batch size is also adjusted to match the number of input sources.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\nprint(\\\"Creating nvinfer (PGIE) \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n# Create nvinfer element\\npgie = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvinfer\\\", \\\"primary-inference\\\")\\nif not pgie:\\n    sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create pgie \\\\n\\\")\\n    \\n# Set nvinfer properties\\npgie.set_property('config-file-path', pgie_config_file)\\npgie_batch_size=pgie.get_property(\\\"batch-size\\\")\\n\\nif(pgie_batch_size != number_sources):\\n    print(\\\"WARNING: Overriding infer-config batch-size\\\",pgie_batch_size,\\\" with number of sources \\\", number_sources,\\\" \\\\n\\\")\\n    pgie.set_property(\\\"batch-size\\\",number_sources)\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Attach Probe for Metadata Extraction in DeepStream Python\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/notebooks/deepstream_launchpad.ipynb\\n\\nAttaches a probe function to a specific element's sink pad to extract metadata generated by GIEs before buffer processing by the tiler. This is crucial for accessing inference results.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\n# We add probe to the sink pad of the tiler element, since by that time, \\n# the buffer would have had gotten all the metadata.\\n\\n# Retrieve the sink pad of the tiler\\ntilersinkpad = tiler.get_static_pad(\\\"sink\\\")\\nif not tilersinkpad:\\n    sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to get sink pad of tiler \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n# Add probe function to the sink pad of the tiler\\ntilersinkpad.add_probe(Gst.PadProbeType.BUFFER, tiler_sink_pad_buffer_probe, 0)\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Access DeepStream Batch Metadata from GStreamer Buffer in Python\\n\\nSource: https://context7.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/llms.txt\\n\\nThis function demonstrates how to retrieve and iterate through batch metadata from a GStreamer buffer within a DeepStream pipeline's pad probe callback. It accesses frame metadata, including the frame number and the count of detected objects. Dependencies include the 'pyds' and 'gi.repository.Gst' libraries.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\nimport pyds\\nfrom gi.repository import Gst\\n\\ndef osd_sink_pad_buffer_probe(pad, info, u_data):\\n    gst_buffer = info.get_buffer()\\n    if not gst_buffer:\\n        print(\\\"Unable to get GstBuffer\\\")\\n        return Gst.PadProbeReturn.OK\\n\\n    # Retrieve batch metadata - hash(gst_buffer) gets C address\\n    batch_meta = pyds.gst_buffer_get_nvds_batch_meta(hash(gst_buffer))\\n\\n    # Iterate through frame metadata list\\n    l_frame = batch_meta.frame_meta_list\\n    while l_frame is not None:\\n        try:\\n            frame_meta = pyds.NvDsFrameMeta.cast(l_frame.data)\\n            print(f\\\"Frame Number: {frame_meta.frame_num}\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"Objects detected: {frame_meta.num_obj_meta}\\\")\\n            l_frame = l_frame.next\\n        except StopIteration:\\n            break\\n\\n    return Gst.PadProbeReturn.OK\\n\\n# Attach probe to OSD sink pad\\nosdsinkpad = nvosd.get_static_pad(\\\"sink\\\")\\nosdsinkpad.add_probe(Gst.PadProbeType.BUFFER, osd_sink_pad_buffer_probe, 0)\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\"}]}"
}

{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Configuring Gst-nvstreammux Properties\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/text/DS_plugin_gst-nvstreammux\\n\\nIllustrates setting key properties of the Gst-nvstreammux plugin to control batch size, timeout, output resolution, padding, and GPU usage.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;c\\n#include \u003cgst/gst.h\u003e\\n\\n// ... assuming muxer is a GstElement pointer to nvstreammux ...\\n\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"batch-size\\\", 8, NULL);\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"batched-push-timeout\\\", 30000000, NULL); // 30ms in nanoseconds\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"width\\\", 1920, NULL);\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"height\\\", 1080, NULL);\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"enable-padding\\\", TRUE, NULL);\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"gpu-id\\\", 0, NULL); // Use GPU 0\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"live-source\\\", TRUE, NULL); // Inform muxer sources are live\\ng_object_set(muxer, \\\"attach-sys-ts\\\", TRUE, NULL); // Use system timestamps\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Add DeepStream Element with Properties in Python\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/text/DS_service_maker_python_into_to_pipeline_api\\n\\nIllustrates how to add a DeepStream element, specifically 'nvstreammux', to a pipeline instance using the 'add' method in Python. It shows how to provide element properties such as 'batch-size', 'width', and 'height' directly as arguments.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\nPipeline pipeline(\\\"sample-pipeline\\\")\\n# nvstreammux is the factory name in Deepstream to create a streammuxer Element\\n# mux is the name of the Element instance\\n# multiple key value pairs can be appended to configure the added Element\\npipeline.add(\\\"nvstreammux\\\", \\\"mux\\\", \\\"batch-size\\\", 1, \\\"width\\\", 1280, \\\"height\\\", 720)\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Gst-nvstreammux Plugin Configuration and Usage\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/text/DS_plugin_Intro\\n\\nDetails the Gst-nvstreammux plugin, responsible for multiplexing multiple video streams into a single batch. Covers configuration properties, tuning solutions, and use cases for video and audio muxing. It also addresses known issues and troubleshooting steps.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\\n/* Example nvstreammux configuration properties */\\n\\n[property]\\nsrc.module = nvstreammux\\nnum-sources = 4\\nwidth = 1920\\nheight = 1080\\nbatch-size = 4\\n\\n/* For heterogeneous batching, enable it and configure */\\n# enable-heterogeneous-batching = true\\n# max-latency = 0\\n\\n/* For adaptive batching, enable it and configure */\\n# enable-adaptive-batching = true\\n# max-latency = 0\\n# max-same-source-buffer = 30\\n\\n/* Other useful properties */\\n# nvbuf-memory-type = 0  (0:Default, 1:CUDA Host, 2:CUDA Device, 3:Shared Memory)\\n# display-text = 1 (Enable display of stream IDs and timestamps)\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Gst-nvstreammux Plugin Features\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/text/DS_plugin_gst-nvstreammux\\n\\nSummary of the Gst-nvstreammux plugin's capabilities, including configurable batch size, multi-stream support, scaling options, and multi-GPU acceleration.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;APIDOC\\n## Gst-nvstreammux Plugin Features\\n\\nThis section summarizes the key features of the &#96;Gst-nvstreammux&#96; plugin.\\n\\n### Method\\nN/A (Feature list)\\n\\n### Endpoint\\nN/A (Plugin features)\\n\\n### Features Summary\\n- **Configurable batch size**: Supports setting a custom number of frames per batch.\\n- **Configurable batching timeout**: Allows defining a timeout for batch formation.\\n- **Multiple input streams with different resolutions**: Can handle streams with varying input resolutions.\\n- **Multiple input streams with different frame rates**: Supports streams with different frame rates.\\n- **Scales to user-determined resolution**: Can scale input frames to a specified output resolution.\\n- **Scales while maintaining aspect ratio with padding**: Preserves aspect ratio by adding black borders during scaling.\\n- **Multi-GPU support**: Capable of utilizing multiple GPUs for processing.\\n- **Input stream DRC support**: Dynamic Range Compression support for input streams (DS 3.0).\\n- **User-configurable CUDA memory type**: Allows selection of memory type for output buffers (Pinned/Device/Unified) (DS 3.0).\\n- **Custom EOS message**: Provides a custom message to the application upon EOS from individual sources (DS 3.0).\\n- **Runtime sinkpad addition/deletion**: Supports dynamically adding/deleting input sources and notifying downstream components (DS 3.0).\\n- **RGBA data handling**: Supports processing and outputting RGBA data (DS 3.0).\\n\\n### Request Example\\nN/A\\n\\n### Response\\nN/A\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### Gst-nvstreammux New\\n\\nSource: https://docs.nvidia.com/metropolis/deepstream/dev-guide/text/DS_plugin_gst-nvstreammux2\\n\\nThe Gst-nvstreammux plugin forms a batch of frames from multiple input sources. When connecting a source to nvstreammux (the muxer), a new pad must be requested from the muxer using &#96;gst_element_get_request_pad()&#96; and the pad template &#96;sink_%u&#96;. For more information, see &#96;link_element_to_streammux_sink_pad()&#96; in the DeepStream app source code. The muxer forms a batched buffer of batch-size frames. (batch-size is specified using the gst object property.) The muxer forwards the frames from that source as a part of the muxer’s output batched buffer. The frames are returned to the source when muxer gets back its output buffer.\"}]}"
}
